name: Caribou Biosciences, Inc.
business: Caribou Biosciences is a clinical-stage CRISPR genome-editing biopharmaceutical company developing transformative allogeneic CAR-T cell therapies for patients with devastating diseases. Its product candidates are manufactured in advance from healthy donor cells to enable broad patient access, rapid treatment, and increased manufacturing scale. The company's current focus is on two clinical-stage allogeneic CAR-T cell therapies targeting CD19 (vispa-cel) and BCMA (CB-011) for hematologic malignancies.
sellsTo: patients with relapsed or refractory B cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma
patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats (CRISPR) genome-editing biopharmaceutical company dedicated to developing transformative therapies for patients with devastating diseases.

tamClaims.claim: NHL is the most common hematologic malignancy with an estimated 80,350 individuals diagnosed in the United States in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: NHL is the most common hematologic malignancy with an estimated 80,350 individuals, or approximately 4% of all cancers, diagnosed in the United States in 2025 according to the National Cancer Institute SEER database.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 34,000 people with LBCL were treated with first-line therapies and approximately 12,000 patients received second-line treatments in the United States in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States in 2025, approximately 34,000 people with LBCL were treated with first-line therapies and approximately 12,000 patients received second-line treatments.

tamClaims.claim: There were an estimated 36,110 new cases of multiple myeloma in 2025 in the United States with an estimated 12,030 deaths.
tamClaims.sentence: there were an estimated 36,110 new cases in 2025 in the United States with an estimated 12,030 deaths in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 60% of the 12,000 second-line LBCL patients treated annually in the United States are ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant and autologous CAR-T cell therapy.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe there is a meaningful commercial opportunity within this population, as approximately 60% of the 12,000 2L LBCL patients treated annually in the United States are considered ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant and autologous CAR-T cell therapy.
